task_completion.description: Open the original floorplan.dxf file in LibreCAD
task_completion.visual_evidence: The LibreCAD application window is open and displays an architectural floorplan. The window title or file tab should indicate 'floorplan.dxf'.
The Layer List panel shows a layer named 'SAFETY'. The layer properties should indicate the color Red and a line width of 0.50mm (often visible as an icon or in layer settings).
A red line path is visible on the floorplan. It should start at a specific point, go straight up, and then turn right (forming an inverted 'L' shape or two connected segments).

task_completion.description: Draw the 'You Are Here' circle and fire extinguisher square
task_completion.visual_evidence: A red circle is drawn at the starting point of the evacuation route. A red square is drawn to the right of the circle, horizontally aligned with the circle's center.

task_completion.description: Add the required text labels for the safety elements
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'YOU ARE HERE' is visible near the red circle. The text 'EXIT' is visible near the end of the red route path. The text 'FE' is visible near the red square.

task_completion.description: Save the updated drawing as evacuation_plan.dxf
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Save As' dialog is used to save the file as 'evacuation_plan.dxf', or the LibreCAD window title updates to show 'evacuation_plan.dxf'.

integrity.description: Agent used the LibreCAD GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking on LibreCAD tools (line, circle, rectangle, text), using the layer panel, and interacting with the drawing canvas.

integrity.description: Agent did not use a terminal script to bypass GUI drawing
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal is not used to run a Python script (e.g., using ezdxf) that directly generates the output DXF file without manual drawing.

integrity.description: Agent shows step-by-step progress of drawing the elements
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show intermediate states where elements are added one by one, or the LibreCAD command line at the bottom shows drawing commands being executed.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a new layer named 'SAFETY' with Color: Red (DXF color code 1) and Line Width: 0.50mm. The evacuation route goes from (300, 300) UP to (300, 600) and RIGHT to (800, 600). The 'You Are Here' marker is a circle at (300, 300) with radius 25. The fire extinguisher is a 50x50 square centered at (500, 300). Required text labels are 'YOU ARE HERE', 'EXIT', and 'FE'. The final file must be saved as '/home/ga/Documents/LibreCAD/evacuation_plan.dxf'.
